summary: DHH expresses enthusiasm for UserLand's upcoming Radio 8.0 release, a blogging and RSS tool. He had used version 7 and appreciated its RSS subscription features but found the outliner lacking, switching to NoteMap instead. With 13 months of development by Dave Winer's team, he's ready to try Radio again. He's particularly excited about the multiple blogs feature being teased by Robert Scoble.
key_points: Radio 7's RSS features were strong but its outliner was insufficient, leading DHH to use NoteMap instead
Radio 8.0 represents over a year of development effort from UserLand/Dave Winer
The multiple blogs feature is highlighted as a compelling new capability
money_quotes: I have the 7th version installed, and loved the RSS subscription features, but the outliner left me wanting more
Now with 13 months of development from Winer and Co. put into Radio 8, I'm most certainly going to give it another go
key_insight: An early glimpse of DHH's engagement with the blogging tools ecosystem, showing his interest in RSS and publishing platforms years before creating Ruby on Rails.
